ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the employee */,
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job title of the employee */,
  "degree_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the degree obtained by the employee */,
  "salary" REAL /* Current salary of the employee */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Birthdate of the employee */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the employee */,
  "joining_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee joined the company */,
  "leaving_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee leaves the company (if applicable) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current employment status of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_degree_id FOREIGN KEY ("degree_id") REFERENCES degrees ("degree_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_title_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_title_id") REFERENCES job_titles ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_titles" (
  "job_title_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Name of the job title */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the job title */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the job title */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_title_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "degrees" (
  "degree_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each degree */,
  "degree_name" TEXT /* Name of the degree */,
  "field_of_study" TEXT /* Field of study for the degree */,
  "institute_name" TEXT /* Name of the institute where the degree was obtained */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("degree_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salary_history" (
  "salary_history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary change record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee whose salary is being tracked */,
  "previous_salary" REAL /* Previous salary before the change */,
  "new_salary" REAL /* New salary after the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the salary change occurred */,
  "reason_for_change" TEXT /* Reason for the salary change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_salary_history_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., HR Manager, Team Lead) */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee linked to the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Last login date and time for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_qualifications" (
  "qualification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each qualification */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who holds the qualification */,
  "qualification_name" TEXT /* Name of the qualification */,
  "issue_date" TEXT /* Date when the qualification was obtained */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the qualification expires (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("qualification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_qualifications_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_benefits" (
  "benefit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each benefit */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee who receives the benefit */,
  "benefit_name" TEXT /* Name of the benefit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the benefit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("benefit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_benefits_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
